title: SQL Interview Practice: 10 Real SQL Challenges
content:
### Day 60: SQL Interview Practice: 10 Real SQL Challenges

**SQL Interview Tips:**
1. Always clarify the schema first
2. Start simple, add complexity
3. Use CTEs for readability
4. Think about edge cases (NULLs, duplicates)
5. Practice writing without an IDE
codeExample:
# Day 60: SQL Interview Practice: 10 Real SQL Challenges
# SQL Interview Challenges
sql = """
-- 1. Second highest salary
SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees
WHERE salary < (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees);

-- 2. Employees earning more than their manager
SELECT e.name FROM employees e
JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.id
WHERE e.salary > m.salary;

-- 3. Duplicate emails
SELECT email, COUNT(*) FROM users
GROUP BY email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

-- 4. Customers who never ordered
SELECT c.name FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.id IS NULL;

-- 5. Running total
SELECT date, amount,
    SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY date) AS running_total
FROM sales;
"""
print(sql)
